title: Arbitrage execution sends the swap transaction with skipPreflight:true, disabling simulation so failing/losing arb txs land silently

params.passDetail: sendTransaction runs preflight simulation, so a transaction that would fail (e.g. slippage exceeded, insufficient balance) is rejected client-side before it is submitted and lands on chain.
params.failDetail: skipPreflight:true disables the preflight simulation, so an arbitrage transaction that would revert or execute at a loss is broadcast blindly; the bot never learns it failed and cannot react, committing funds to an unconfirmed/losing state.
params.absentCallDetail: No sendTransaction call found; nothing to check.
params.absentArgDetail: sendTransaction called without an options object; preflight defaults to enabled, which is safe.

provenance.note: Arbitrage bots disable preflight for latency, but on the execution send it means a slippage-failed or already-arbitraged (losing) transaction is broadcast blind with no way to detect the failed/unconfirmed outcome.
